question_text: A student burns 500 J of chemical energy stored in a piece of wood in a campfire. Calculate how much total energy (in joules) is released as heat and light combined, assuming no energy escapes in any other form.
explanation: According to the law of conservation of energy, all the chemical energy stored in the wood must be transferred somewhere. Since no energy escapes in other forms, all 500 J is transferred to heat and light energy combined.
